session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the key information. Let me compile a comprehensive report for the user:
## Remote Dispatch Implementation Reference
Here's the complete breakdown of the implementation components for remote dispatch in `pr dispatch`:
### 1. **IssueInfo** (No `head_ref_name`) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/types.py`
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class IssueInfo: """Information about a GitHub issue.""" number: int title: str body: str state: str # "OPEN" or "CLOSED" url: str labels: list[str] assignees: list[str] created_at: datetime updated_at: datetime author: str # GitHub login of the issue creator ```
**Note:** `IssueInfo` does NOT have `head_ref_name`. This is only on PR objects. For remote dispatch, fetch the PR using `RemoteGitHub.get_issue()` which returns `IssueInfo | IssueNotFound`, but PRs need the full `RemoteGitHub` read methods that work with PR-specific fields.
---
### 2. **RemoteGitHub ABC** (Gateway for no-repo operations) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`
Key methods for remote dispatch:
```python class RemoteGitHub(ABC):
# Read operations @abstractmethod def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user.""" ...
@abstractmethod def get_default_branch_name(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str: """Get the default branch name for a repository.""" ...
@abstractmethod def get_default_branch_sha(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str: """Get the SHA of the default branch HEAD.""" ...
@abstractmethod def get_issue( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, number: int, ) -> IssueInfo | IssueNotFound: """Fetch issue data by number (works for PRs too).""" ...
# Write operations @abstractmethod def create_ref(self, *, owner: str, repo: str, ref: str, sha: str) -> None: """Create a git reference (branch).""" ...
@abstractmethod def create_file_commit( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, path: str, content: str, message: str, branch: str, ) -> str: """Create a file commit using the GitHub Contents API.""" ...
@abstractmethod def create_pull_request( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, head: str, base: str, title: str, body: str, draft: bool, ) -> int: """Create a pull request. Returns PR number.""" ...
@abstractmethod def update_pull_request_body( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, pr_number: int, body: str, ) -> None: """Update a pull request's body.""" ...
@abstractmethod def add_labels( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, issue_number: int, labels: tuple[str, ...], ) -> None: """Add labels to an issue or PR.""" ...
@abstractmethod def dispatch_workflow( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, workflow: str, ref: str, inputs: dict[str, str], ) -> str: """Dispatch a workflow and poll for the run ID. Returns run ID string.""" ...
@abstractmethod def add_issue_comment( self, *, owner: str, repo: str, issue_number: int, body: str, ) -> int: """Add a comment to an issue or PR. Returns comment ID.""" ... ```
---
### 3. **ValidatedPlannedPR** (Local PR validation result) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class ValidatedPlannedPR: """Draft PR that passed all validation checks."""
number: int title: str url: str branch_name: str ```
Created by `_validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch()` which: - Fetches the PR via local `ctx.github.get_pr()` - Checks for `erk-plan` label - Validates PR is OPEN state - Extracts `head_ref_name` from the PR details
---
### 4. **build_impl_context_files()** (File generator, no disk I/O) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py`
```python def build_impl_context_files( plan_content: str, plan_id: str, url: str, *, provider: str, objective_id: int | None, now_iso: str, node_ids: tuple[str, ...] | None, ) -> dict[str, str]: """Build impl-context file contents as an in-memory mapping.
Returns the same content as create_impl_context but without writing to disk, suitable for use with commit_files_to_branch.
Returns: Mapping of relative file paths to string content: { ".erk/impl-context/plan.md": "<plan content>", ".erk/impl-context/ref.json": "{...}" } """ ref_data: dict[str, str | int | list[str] | None] = { "provider": provider, "plan_id": plan_id, "url": url, "created_at": now_iso, "synced_at": now_iso, "labels": [], "objective_id": objective_id, "node_ids": list(node_ids) if node_ids is not None else None, } return { f"{IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR}/plan.md": plan_content, f"{IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR}/ref.json": json.dumps(ref_data, indent=2), } ```
**Note:** `IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR = ".erk/impl-context"` (constant from `planned_pr_lifecycle.py`)
---
### 5. **extract_plan_content()** (Plan content extractor) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr_lifecycle.py`
```python def extract_plan_content(pr_body: str) -> str: """Extract plan content from a PR body at any lifecycle stage.
Looks for <details>/<summary>original-plan</summary> tags first. Falls back to old flat format for backward compatibility.
Args: pr_body: Full PR body string
Returns: Plan content portion of the body """ # Try new <details>-tagged format first DETAILS_OPEN = "<details>\n<summary>original-plan</summary>\n\n" DETAILS_CLOSE = "\n\n</details>"
for details_open in (DETAILS_OPEN, _LEGACY_DETAILS_OPEN): open_idx = pr_body.find(details_open) if open_idx != -1: content_start = open_idx + len(details_open) close_idx = pr_body.find(DETAILS_CLOSE, content_start) if close_idx != -1: return pr_body[content_start:close_idx]
# Backward compat: old flat format PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR = "\n\n---\n\n" separator_index = pr_body.find(PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR) if separator_index == -1: return pr_body candidate_prefix = pr_body[:separator_index] if "<!-- erk:metadata-block:" not in candidate_prefix: return pr_body return pr_body[separator_index + len(PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR) :] ```
**No equivalent `extract_plan_from_body()` function exists.** Use `extract_plan_content()` from `planned_pr_lifecycle.py` instead.
---
### 6. **repo_option and resolve_owner_repo()** (Repository resolution) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py`
```python def resolve_owner_repo( ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None, ) -> tuple[str, str]: """Resolve owner/repo from --repo flag or local git context.
Args: ctx: ErkContext target_repo: Optional "owner/repo" string from --repo flag
Returns: Tuple of (owner, repo_name)
Raises: UserFacingCliError: If owner/repo cannot be determined """ if target_repo is not None: if "/" not in target_repo or target_repo.count("/") != 1: raise UserFacingCliError( f"Invalid --repo format: '{target_repo}'\n" "Expected format: owner/repo (e.g., dagster-io/erk)" ) owner, repo_name = target_repo.split("/") return (owner, repo_name)
if isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) or ctx.repo.github is None: raise UserFacingCliError( "Cannot determine target repository.\n" "Use --repo owner/repo or run from inside a git repository." ) return (ctx.repo.github.owner, ctx.repo.github.repo)
def get_remote_github(ctx: ErkContext) -> RemoteGitHub: """Get or construct a RemoteGitHub from context.
Uses ctx.remote_github if provided (tests inject FakeRemoteGitHub here). Otherwise constructs RealRemoteGitHub from ctx.http_client. """ if ctx.remote_github is not None: return ctx.remote_github
if ctx.http_client is None: raise UserFacingCliError( "GitHub authentication required.\nRun 'gh auth login' to authenticate." )
return RealRemoteGitHub(http_client=ctx.http_client, time=ctx.time)
# Click option for --repo flag repo_option = click.option( "--repo", "target_repo", type=str, default=None, help="Target repo (owner/repo) for remote operation without local git clone", ) ```
---
### 7. **dispatch_one_shot_remote.py** (Canonical remote dispatch pattern) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py`
This is the existing implementation showing the full remote dispatch flow:
```python def dispatch_one_shot_remote( *, remote: RemoteGitHub, owner: str, repo: str, params: OneShotDispatchParams, dry_run: bool, ref: str | None, time_gateway: Time, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None, ) -> OneShotDispatchResult | None: """Execute the full remote dispatch sequence for a one-shot workflow.
Creates branch, commits prompt file, creates draft PR, and dispatches workflow — all via GitHub REST API without a local git clone. """ # 1. Get authenticated user submitted_by = remote.get_authenticated_user()
# 2. Detect trunk branch trunk = remote.get_default_branch_name(owner=owner, repo=repo)
# 3. Generate branch name (for timestamp formatting) branch_name = generate_planned_pr_branch_name(...)
# 4. Get trunk SHA for branch creation trunk_sha = remote.get_default_branch_sha(owner=owner, repo=repo)
# 5. Create branch from trunk remote.create_ref( owner=owner, repo=repo, ref=f"refs/heads/{branch_name}", sha=trunk_sha, )
# 6. Write prompt to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md remote.create_file_commit( owner=owner, repo=repo, path=".erk/impl-context/prompt.md", content=params.prompt + "\n", message=f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:60]}", branch=branch_name, )
# 7. Create draft PR with metadata header pr_number = remote.create_pull_request( owner=owner, repo=repo, head=branch_name, base=trunk, title=pr_title, body=pr_body_initial, draft=True, )
# 8. Add footer now that we have the PR number footer = build_pr_body_footer(pr_number) remote.update_pull_request_body( owner=owner, repo=repo, pr_number=pr_number, body=pr_body_initial + footer, )
# 9. Add plan labels remote.add_labels( owner=owner, repo=repo, issue_number=pr_number, labels=("erk-pr", "erk-plan"), )
# 10. Dispatch workflow run_id = remote.dispatch_workflow( owner=owner, repo=repo, workflow=ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW, ref=dispatch_ref, inputs=inputs, )
# 11. Post queued comment (best-effort) remote.add_issue_comment( owner=owner, repo=repo, issue_number=pr_number, body=comment_body, )
return OneShotDispatchResult( pr_number=pr_number, run_id=run_id, branch_name=branch_name, ) ```
**Key patterns from this implementation:** - All operations use explicit `owner`, `repo` parameters - No local git clone required - Metadata block created with `format_plan_header_body()` before PR creation - PR footer added AFTER PR creation (because footer needs PR number) - Labels added immediately after footer update - Workflow dispatch with `ref` parameter (defaults to trunk if None) - Post-dispatch operations are best-effort with error handling
---
### 8. **NoRepoSentinel** (Sentinel for non-repo contexts) **File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py`
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class NoRepoSentinel: """Sentinel value indicating execution outside a git repository.
Used when commands run outside git repositories (e.g., before init, in non-git directories). Commands that require repo context can check for this sentinel and fail fast. """
message: str = "Not inside a git repository" ```
**Usage in context helpers:** ```python def require_repo_root(ctx: click.Context) -> Path: """Get repo root from context, exiting with error if not initialized.""" # ... repo = ctx.obj.repo if isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel): click.echo("Error: Not in a git repository", err=True) raise SystemExit(1)
return repo.root ```
**In repo resolution:** ```python def resolve_owner_repo(ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None) -> tuple[str, str]: # ... if isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) or ctx.repo.github is None: raise UserFacingCliError( "Cannot determine target repository.\n" "Use --repo owner/repo or run from inside a git repository." ) return (ctx.repo.github.owner, ctx.repo.github.repo) ```
---
### Summary of Key Differences for Remote Dispatch
**vs. Local Dispatch:** 1. Use `RemoteGitHub` instead of `LocalGitHub` (no `repo_root` parameter) 2. Explicitly pass `owner`, `repo` to all gateway methods 3. Use `build_impl_context_files()` to generate file content without disk I/O 4. Pass file content dict to `commit_files_to_branch()` (git plumbing) 5. Use `repo_option` decorator and `resolve_owner_repo()` for CLI arguments 6. Check for `NoRepoSentinel` when working without local git context
---
All absolute file paths: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/types.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_context.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr_lifecycle.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py`
